title: Classifying Academic Papers
text: cat.ademic thin domain wrapper around cat.stack adds OpenAlex-based paper fetching plus academic prompt framing. can: Fetch papers journal topic via OpenAlex (journal_name, journal_issn, journal_field, topic_name, topic_id) classify one call. Classify text already (abstracts, full text, PDFs) plain character vector file directory. Everything else — supported models, output format, ensemble voting, batch mode — identical cat.stack.

title: Classifying Academic Papers
Classifying Academic Papers
code: abstracts <- c(   "We use mixed-methods to study labor market outcomes for...",   "This paper develops a formal model of bargaining under...",   "A systematic review of 47 studies on educational interventions..." )  results <- classify(   categories = c("Empirical-quantitative",                  "Empirical-qualitative",                  "Theoretical-formal",                  "Review/meta-analysis",                  "Other"),   input_data = abstracts,   mode       = "text",   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model = "gpt-4o-mini" )

title: Classifying Academic Papers
text: cat.ademic connects OpenAlex — free, open scholarly database — fetch papers journal, field, topic. Set polite_email (email) higher rate limits. ISSN unambiguous journal identification:
code: results <- classify(   categories   = c("Quantitative", "Qualitative", "Mixed Methods"),   journal_name = "American Sociological Review",   paper_limit  = 100L,   date_from    = "2024-01-01",   polite_email = "you@university.edu",   api_key      = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") ) results <- classify(   categories   = c("Empirical", "Theoretical", "Review"),   journal_issn = "0003-1224",                # AJS   paper_limit  = 50L,   polite_email = "you@university.edu",   api_key      = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: Classifying Academic Papers
text: OpenAlex auto-tags papers research topics. can pull papers topic across journals:
code: results <- classify(   categories   = c("Causal-identification", "Descriptive",                    "Theoretical", "Other"),   topic_name   = "climate change adaptation",   paper_limit  = 200L,   date_from    = "2023-01-01",   polite_email = "you@university.edu",   api_key      = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: Classifying Academic Papers
text: Pass directory vector file paths. cat.ademic extracts text (renders pages images vision models) classifies:
code: # One-time: install PDF extras # cat.stack::install_cat_stack(pdf = TRUE)  results <- classify(   categories = c("Has-DGP-assumption", "No-DGP-assumption",                  "Unclear", "Other"),   input_data = "./papers/",                  # directory of PDFs   mode       = "image",                      # rendered-page vision mode   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model = "gpt-4o"                      # vision-capable model )

title: Classifying Academic Papers
text: long full-text inputs, summarizing first can improve downstream classification quality (reduce token cost):
code: summaries <- summarize(   input_data   = "./papers/",   description  = "Sociology articles",   instructions = "Summarize methodology and key findings in 3 sentences",   format       = "paragraph",   api_key      = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model   = "gpt-4o-mini" )  results <- classify(   categories = c("Causal", "Descriptive", "Theoretical", "Other"),   input_data = summaries$summary,   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: Classifying Academic Papers
text: Always set polite_email fetching OpenAlex — without ’re throttled low rate limit. Abstract vs. full text. Abstracts cheap fast; full-text classification (PDF input) accurate methodological categories costs . Use abstracts screening, full text -depth coding. Cite OpenAlex publish data fetched — see openalex.org citation guidance.

title: Classifying Academic Papers
text: Full Getting Started guide: vignette("getting-started", package = "cat.llm") Per-function reference: ?cat.ademic::classify, ?cat.ademic::extract, ?cat.ademic::explore, ?cat.ademic::summarize OpenAlex docs: https://docs.openalex.org

what: cat.ademic — Academic paper classification for R
title: cat.ademic — Academic paper classification for R
text: Academic paper classification LLMs. domain wrapper around cat.stack adds journal topic sourcing parameters classifying, extracting, exploring, summarizing academic literature. cat.ademic wraps Python catademic package via reticulate.

title: cat.ademic — Academic paper classification for R
code: # From R-universe (recommended) install.packages("cat.ademic",                  repos = c("https://chrissoria.r-universe.dev",                           "https://cloud.r-project.org"))  # Or from a local clone devtools::install("path/to/cat.stack") devtools::install("path/to/cat.ademic")  # Install the Python backend (one-time setup) # pip install cat-ademic

title: cat.ademic — Academic paper classification for R
code: library(cat.ademic)  results <- classify(   categories   = c("Quantitative", "Qualitative", "Mixed Methods"),   journal_name = "American Sociological Review",   paper_limit  = 100L,   polite_email = "you@university.edu",   api_key      = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: cat.ademic — Academic paper classification for R
code: result <- extract(   topic_name   = "climate change adaptation",   paper_limit  = 200L,   polite_email = "you@university.edu",   api_key      = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") ) print(result$top_categories)

title: cat.ademic — Academic paper classification for R
code: results <- summarize(   input_data   = df$abstracts,   description  = "Sociology journal abstracts",   instructions = "Summarize the key findings in 2 sentences",   api_key      = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: cat.ademic — Academic paper classification for R
text: MIT

what: Classify academic papers using LLMs — classify
title: Classify academic papers using LLMs — classify
text: Wraps Python catademic.classify() function. Adds journal topic sourcing parameters base cat.stack classification engine.

title: Classify academic papers using LLMs — classify
code: classify(   categories,   input_data = NULL,   api_key = NULL,   journal_issn = NULL,   journal_name = NULL,   journal_field = NULL,   topic_name = NULL,   topic_id = NULL,   paper_limit = 50L,   date_from = NULL,   date_to = NULL,   polite_email = NULL,   journal = NULL,   field = NULL,   research_focus = NULL,   paper_metadata = NULL,   description = "",   filename = NULL,   save_directory = NULL,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   mode = "image",   creativity = NULL,   safety = FALSE,   chain_of_verification = FALSE,   chain_of_thought = FALSE,   step_back_prompt = FALSE,   context_prompt = FALSE,   thinking_budget = 0L,   example1 = NULL,   example2 = NULL,   example3 = NULL,   example4 = NULL,   example5 = NULL,   example6 = NULL,   model_source = "auto",   max_categories = 12L,   categories_per_chunk = 10L,   divisions = 10L,   research_question = NULL,   models = NULL,   consensus_threshold = "unanimous",   use_json_schema = TRUE,   max_workers = NULL,   fail_strategy = "partial",   max_retries = 5L,   batch_retries = 2L,   retry_delay = 1,   row_delay = 0,   pdf_dpi = 150L,   auto_download = FALSE,   add_other = "prompt",   check_verbosity = TRUE,   prompt_tune = NULL,   tune_iterations = 1L,   tune_ui = "browser",   tune_optimize = "balanced" )

title: Classify academic papers using LLMs — classify
text: categories character vector category names, "auto". input_data character vector, list, data.frame column, NULL fetch academic sources. Default NULL. api_key Character NULL. API key LLM provider. journal_issn Character NULL. Journal ISSN fetch papers . journal_name Character NULL. Journal name fetch papers . journal_field Character NULL. Academic field filter . topic_name Character NULL. Topic name search . topic_id Character NULL. OpenAlex topic ID. paper_limit Integer. Max papers fetch. Default 50L. date_from Character NULL. Start date (YYYY-MM-DD). date_to Character NULL. End date (YYYY-MM-DD). polite_email Character NULL. Email polite API pool. journal Character NULL. Alias journal_name. field Character NULL. Alias journal_field. research_focus Character NULL. Research focus filter. paper_metadata Named list NULL. Additional paper metadata. description Character. Context description. Default "". filename Character NULL. Output CSV filename. save_directory Character NULL. Output directory. user_model Character. Model name. Default "gpt-4o". mode Character. Processing mode. Default "image". creativity Numeric NULL. Temperature. Default NULL. safety Logical. Save progress item. Default FALSE. chain_of_verification Logical. Default FALSE. chain_of_thought Logical. Default FALSE. step_back_prompt Logical. Default FALSE. context_prompt Logical. Default FALSE. thinking_budget Integer. Default 0L. example1, example2, example3, example4, example5, example6 Optional -shot examples. model_source Character. Provider hint. Default "auto". max_categories Integer. Default 12L. categories_per_chunk Integer. Default 10L. divisions Integer. Default 10L. research_question Character NULL. Optional research context. models List model specs ensemble mode. consensus_threshold Character numeric. Default "unanimous". use_json_schema Logical. Default TRUE. max_workers Integer NULL. Default NULL. fail_strategy Character. Default "partial". max_retries Integer. Default 5L. batch_retries Integer. Default 2L. retry_delay Numeric. Default 1.0. row_delay Numeric. Default 0.0. pdf_dpi Integer. Default 150L. auto_download Logical. Default FALSE. add_other Logical "prompt". Default "prompt". check_verbosity Logical. Default TRUE. prompt_tune Integer NULL. Rows sampled per APO correction round. Default NULL. tune_iterations Integer. APO optimization passes. Default 1L. tune_ui Character. Correction UI: "browser" "terminal". Default "browser". tune_optimize Character. Metric optimize: "balanced", "sensitivity", "precision". Default "balanced".

title: Classify academic papers using LLMs — classify
text: data.frame classification results.

title: Classify academic papers using LLMs — classify
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Classify abstracts directly results <- classify(   categories = c("Methods", "Theory", "Review", "Other"),   input_data = df$abstract,   mode       = "text",   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model = "gpt-4o-mini" )  # Fetch papers from a journal via OpenAlex results <- classify(   categories   = c("Empirical", "Theoretical", "Review"),   journal_name = "American Sociological Review",   paper_limit  = 100L,   polite_email = "you@university.edu",   api_key      = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") ) } # }

what: Explore raw categories in academic paper data — explore
title: Explore raw categories in academic paper data — explore
text: Wraps Python catademic.explore() function. Returns every category string extracted every chunk across every iteration – duplicates intact.

title: Explore raw categories in academic paper data — explore
code: explore(   input_data = NULL,   api_key = NULL,   description = "",   journal_issn = NULL,   journal_name = NULL,   journal_field = NULL,   topic_name = NULL,   topic_id = NULL,   paper_limit = 50L,   date_from = NULL,   date_to = NULL,   polite_email = NULL,   max_categories = 12L,   categories_per_chunk = 10L,   divisions = 12L,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   creativity = NULL,   specificity = "broad",   research_question = NULL,   filename = NULL,   model_source = "auto",   iterations = 8L,   random_state = NULL,   focus = NULL,   chunk_delay = 0 )

Default "". journal_issn Character NULL. Journal ISSN. journal_name Character NULL. Journal name. journal_field Character NULL. Academic field. topic_name Character NULL. Topic name. topic_id Character NULL. OpenAlex topic ID. paper_limit Integer. Max papers fetch. Default 50L. date_from Character NULL. Start date (YYYY-MM-DD). date_to Character NULL. End date (YYYY-MM-DD). polite_email Character NULL. Email polite API pool. max_categories Integer. Default 12L. categories_per_chunk Integer. Default 10L. divisions Integer. Default 12L. user_model Character. Default "gpt-4o". creativity Numeric NULL. Default NULL. specificity Character. Default "broad". research_question Character NULL. filename Character NULL. model_source Character. Default "auto". iterations Integer. Default 8L. random_state Integer NULL. focus Character NULL. chunk_delay Numeric. Default 0.0.

text: character vector every category string extracted.

title: Explore raw categories in academic paper data — explore
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ raw_cats <- explore(   input_data = df$abstracts,   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model = "gpt-4o-mini",   iterations = 4L ) table(raw_cats) } # }

what: Extract categories from academic papers using LLMs — extract
title: Extract categories from academic papers using LLMs — extract
text: Wraps Python catademic.extract() function. Discovers returns normalised, deduplicated set categories academic paper data.

what: Summarize academic papers using LLMs — summarize
title: Summarize academic papers using LLMs — summarize
text: Wraps Python catademic.summarize() function. Generates summaries academic paper data. Python function accepts input_data passes arguments via **kwargs cat_stack.summarize().

title: Summarize academic papers using LLMs — summarize
code: summarize(   input_data,   api_key = NULL,   description = "",   instructions = "",   format = "paragraph",   max_length = NULL,   focus = NULL,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   model_source = "auto",   mode = "image",   input_mode = NULL,   input_type = "auto",   pdf_dpi = 150L,   creativity = NULL,   thinking_budget = 0L,   chain_of_thought = TRUE,   context_prompt = FALSE,   step_back_prompt = FALSE,   filename = NULL,   save_directory = NULL,   models = NULL,   max_workers = NULL,   parallel = NULL,   auto_download = FALSE,   safety = FALSE,   max_retries = 5L,   batch_retries = 2L,   retry_delay = 1,   row_delay = 0,   fail_strategy = "partial",   batch_mode = FALSE,   batch_poll_interval = 30,   batch_timeout = 86400 )

title: Summarize academic papers using LLMs — summarize
text: input_data character vector, list, data.frame column paper abstracts text. api_key Character NULL. API key model provider. description Character. Context description. Default "". instructions Character. Specific instructions summary. Default "". format Character. Output format. Default "paragraph". max_length Integer NULL. Max summary length. Default NULL. focus Character NULL. Optional focus. Default NULL. user_model Character. Model name. Default "gpt-4o". model_source Character. Provider hint. Default "auto". mode Character. Processing mode. Default "image". input_mode Character NULL. Explicit input mode. Default NULL. input_type Character. Input type. Default "auto". pdf_dpi Integer. DPI PDFs. Default 150L. creativity Numeric NULL. Temperature. Default NULL. thinking_budget Integer. Default 0L. chain_of_thought Logical. Default TRUE. context_prompt Logical. Default FALSE. step_back_prompt Logical. Default FALSE. filename Character NULL. Output filename. save_directory Character NULL. Output directory. models List model specs ensemble mode. Default NULL. max_workers Integer NULL. Default NULL. parallel Logical NULL. Default NULL. auto_download Logical. Default FALSE. safety Logical. Default FALSE. max_retries Integer. Default 5L. batch_retries Integer. Default 2L. retry_delay Numeric. Default 1.0. row_delay Numeric. Default 0.0. fail_strategy Character. Default "partial". batch_mode Logical. Default FALSE. batch_poll_interval Numeric. Default 30.0. batch_timeout Numeric. Default 86400.0.

title: cat.ademic 0.1.0
text: Initial release R interface Python catademic package. Domain wrapper around cat.stack adds journal topic sourcing parameters (via OpenAlex) academic literature analysis. Exports classify(), extract(), explore(), summarize().
